title: Transfer Method of Ambrosia Beetle Sterilization
author: M. Elizabeth Moore

steps.title: Turn BSC on for at least 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Turn BSC on for at least 4 minutes

steps.title: Sterilize surfaces of BSC with 70% Ethanol
steps.instruction: Sterilize surfaces of BSC with 70% Ethanol

steps.title: Label petri dish with the beetle...
steps.instruction: Label petri dish with the beetle information (species, source, date collected, approximate number, etc), transfer number (first dish is T0, second dish is T1, etc), and the details of the next transfer (wet or dry, time of day)

steps.title: Place all materials, including beetles (should...
steps.instruction: Place all materials, including beetles (should be held in a collecting flask with the opening covered), into the BSC

steps.title: Flame sterilize fine forceps using the lighter
steps.instruction: Flame sterilize fine forceps using the lighter

steps.title: Use the sterilized fine forceps to...
steps.instruction: Use the sterilized fine forceps to remove sterile filter paper from container and place into each sterile petri dish

steps.title: For wet transfers, add 1000uL of...
steps.instruction: For wet transfers, add 1000uL of autoclaved DI water to the filter paper in each petri dish (omit this step for dry transfers)

steps.title: Add beetles to petri dish, using...
steps.instruction: Add beetles to petri dish, using flat forceps as needed (beetles can be dumped into the petri dish directly from the flask, and flat forceps used for stragglers).

steps.title: Parafilm petri dish and return to growth chamber
steps.instruction: Parafilm petri dish and return to growth chamber

steps.title: Alternate beetles between wet and dry...
steps.instruction: Alternate beetles between wet and dry petri dishes, starting and ending with a wet dish to prevent desiccation. The standard number of transfers is 3 (wet -- dry -- wet), but more steps can be added if needed.

steps.title: Example
steps.instruction: Example: If beetles are placed in their first wet dish in the afternoon of day 1, transfer to a dry dish the morning of day 2, then transfer to a new wet dish in the afternoon of day 2.

steps.title: Always alternating wet and dry dishes
steps.instruction: Always alternating wet and dry dishes

steps.title: Beetles should end on a wet...
steps.instruction: Beetles should end on a wet transfer so they don’t desiccate while waiting to set up on bolts or artificial media

steps.title: At the end of the transfers,...
steps.instruction: At the end of the transfers, beetles can be held in a wet petri dish for up to a week before establishing on wood or artificial media, however beetles do better if transferred to substrate within 3-4 days. We recommend following the Ambrosia Beetle Surface Sterilization Protocol ( https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x54v9bk44l3e/v1 ) immediately before setting up on wood or media.

steps.title: We have observed that beetle digging...
steps.instruction: We have observed that beetle digging success is strongly correlated with undamaged tarsites, and that leg damage increases with high beetle density. For a standard size petri dish, we recommend a beetle density of 100 beetles or less.
